subject: Computer Networking
title: NFC: Tap to Connect
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on NFC: Tap to Connect.

hook.text: You tap your phone on a payment terminal. Money transfers in a fraction of a second. That's NFC.

buildup.text: NFC works at ranges of just a few centimeters. The proximity itself acts as a security measure.

discovery.text: NFC uses electromagnetic induction. The reader generates a field; the tag or device responds with data.

twist.text: NFC tags are passive — they have no battery. The reader's field powers them. That's why stickers and cards work.

climax.text: Apple Pay, Google Pay, transit cards, and hotel key cards all use NFC. Tap-to-do-anything is now mainstream.

punchline.text: NFC: close enough to touch, fast enough to forget.

quiz.question: How do passive NFC tags work without a battery?
options: They draw power from the reader's electromagnetic field
They store solar energy during the day
They use the phone's battery wirelessly
